title: Fuel Cell System Engineer (f/m/x) – Aircraft Powertrain
jd_text: The DLR Institute of Engineering Thermodynamics develops fuel cell systems for greener aviation. You will design and integrate PEM fuel cell systems and balance-of-plant components for an aircraft powertrain, run system simulations, and support test-bench commissioning. Required: M.Sc. in mechanical/automotive/aerospace engineering, hands-on fuel cell system experience, CAD, system modelling. English working language; German is a plus.

title: Systems Engineer Fuel Cell Stack (m/f/d)
jd_text: cellcentric (Daimler Truck & Volvo JV) develops fuel cell systems for heavy-duty trucks. Responsibilities: stack-level system engineering, balance-of-plant integration, requirements and validation for next-gen fuel cell systems. Required: degree in mechanical/automotive engineering, fuel cell or powertrain experience, MATLAB/Simulink. German B2 preferred.

title: Development Engineer Fuel Cell Power Module (m/f/d)
jd_text: Mobility Solutions: develop fuel cell power modules for commercial vehicles. Tasks: component and system design, DFM/DFMEA, test planning, supplier coordination. Required: engineering degree, fuel cell / electrochemistry knowledge, CAD, design for manufacturing. German and English.

title: Fuel Cell Systems Engineer (m/f/d)
jd_text: H2FLY builds hydrogen-electric aviation powertrains. You will develop and test fuel cell systems and balance-of-plant for flight, support integration and ground/flight testing. Required: M.Sc. engineering, fuel cell system experience, hydrogen handling, test-bench work. English working language.

title: Hydrogen Fuel Cell Propulsion Engineer
jd_text: ZEROe programme: contribute to hydrogen fuel cell propulsion architecture for future aircraft. Tasks: system architecture, sizing, integration trade studies. Required: aerospace/mechanical engineering degree, fuel cell or propulsion systems background. English; German optional.

title: PhD Position (Wissenschaftlicher Mitarbeiter) – Fuel Cell System Control
jd_text: PhD position on model-based control of PEM fuel cell systems for mobility applications. You will develop control strategies, build system models, and validate on test benches, leading to a doctoral degree. Required: M.Sc. in engineering, control/modelling skills, fuel cell interest. German desirable, English accepted.

title: Senior Sales Representative – Marine
jd_text: Drive sales of fuel cell modules into the marine segment. Manage customer relationships, negotiate contracts, hit revenue targets. Required: 8+ years technical sales, marine industry network.
